metadata.name: Wall of Omens blocks and survives — 0/4 Defender blocks 3/3
metadata.description: p2 has a 3/3 Beast token attacking. p1 blocks with Wall of Omens (0/4 Defender). Combat damage: Beast deals 3 to Wall (3 < 4 toughness — Wall survives). Wall deals 0 to Beast (power 0 — no damage). Tests that a high-toughness defender blocks without dying to a smaller attacker, and that 0-power creatures deal 0 damage.
metadata.generation_notes: Wall of Omens is a 0/4 Defender. Beast token is 3/3. In combat: Beast assigns 3 damage to Wall (3 < 4 toughness — Wall survives, no SBA). Wall assigns 0 damage to Beast (0 power). This is a combat interaction. Note: the replay harness does NOT currently support DeclareAttackers/DeclareBlockers commands, so this script is informational (draft). It documents the expected behavior. The actual engine behavior is unit-tested in combat.rs. Setting confidence to medium and review_status to pending_review.

script.step_note: p1 blocks Beast token with Wall of Omens.

actions.description: Initial: Wall of Omens on p1 battlefield, Beast token on p2 battlefield (attacking)
actions.note: Beast token declared as attacker in previous step.

actions.object: Combat Damage — Beast vs Wall of Omens

resolution.note: CR 510.1: Beast assigns 3 damage to Wall. Wall assigns 0 damage to Beast.

actions.note: Combat damage assigned: Beast deals 3 to Wall (Wall survives at 3/4 damage), Wall deals 0 to Beast.
SBAs: Wall has 3 damage but 4 toughness — not lethal (3 < 4). No creatures die.

actions.description: After combat: Wall survives with 3 damage marked; Beast survives with 0 damage; both on battlefield
actions.note: Wall of Omens has 3 damage marked (3 < 4 toughness, survives). Beast has 0 damage.
